info.title: Recycle Bin
info.description: To get, restore and delete Recycle Bin records

get.description: Returns paginated recycle-bin entries. Use filters or ids to scope results.

200.description: Successful response with the requested data.

display_name.description: Display name of the deleted record.

deleted_time.description: Timestamp when the record was deleted.

module.description: Module name of the deleted record.

name.description: Name of the user who deleted.

id.description: Identifier of the user who deleted.
Unique identifier of the recycle bin record.

more_records.description: Indicates if more records are available.

204.description: No content: the request succeeded but there is no data to return.

delete.description: Permanently deletes matching recycle-bin records. This is a destructive operation and may partially succeed for some items; check 207 responses for per-item results.

207.description: Multi-status: some items succeeded while others failed. Check per-item results in the response body.

details.description: Additional details about the operation result

schema.description: Multi-status response containing results for each deletion attempt

400.description: Bad request — invalid parameters or payload. See response body for error details.

post.description: Restores multiple recycle-bin records based on provided criteria. You can specify records to restore using one of: `ids`, `filters`, or `restore_all_records`. Only one of these fields should be provided; supplying multiple will result in an error. When the number of records to restore (including child records) exceeds 1000, the operation is scheduled as a background job (202 status). For 1000 or fewer records, restoration happens immediately (200/207 status).

schema.description: Request payload for restoring recycle bin records.

ids.description: Array of recycle bin record IDs to restore. Mutually exclusive with `filters` and `restore_all_records`.

filters.description: Filter criteria to select records for restoration. Mutually exclusive with `ids` and `restore_all_records`. Only 'AND' group operator is supported.

group_operator.description: Logical operator for combining filter conditions. Only 'AND' is supported.

restore_all_records.description: If set to true, restores all records in the recycle bin. Mutually exclusive with `ids` and `filters`. If set to false, either `ids` or `filters` must be provided.

oneOf.description: Restore all records in the recycle bin.

200.description: Successful response with per-item result(s). Returned when restoration completes immediately (1000 or fewer records including children).

202.description: Accepted — restoration has been scheduled as a background job. Returned when the number of records (including child records) exceeds 1000.

207.description: Multi-status: some items succeeded while others failed. Check per-item results in the response body.

details.description: Additional details about the operation result

schema.description: Multi-status response containing results for each restoration attempt

400.description: Bad request — invalid request payload or ambiguous input. See response body for error details.

get.description: Returns the count of items in the recycle bin. Useful for monitoring or quick summaries.

200.description: Successful response containing the total count of recycle bin items.

post.description: Permanently deletes all records from the Recycle Bin. This action is irreversible, as deleted records cannot be restored after this operation. When the number of records in the Recycle Bin exceeds 1000 (including child records), the deletion will be scheduled as a background job (202 status). If the count is 1000 or fewer, the records are deleted immediately (200 status). Only users with the Admin profile can empty the Recycle Bin.

200.description: Successful response — recycle bin emptied immediately (1000 or fewer records).

schema.description: Success response for empty recycle bin operation

202.description: Accepted — emptying recycle bin has been scheduled as a background job (more than 1000 records including children).

schema.description: Scheduled response for empty recycle bin operation

400.description: Bad request — invalid request method or non-admin user attempting the operation.

post.description: Restores a single recycle-bin record identified by the path parameter `record_id`.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Identifier of the recycle-bin record. Format depends on CRM configuration (e.g., numeric or UUID).

400.description: Bad request — request payload invalid or missing required fields.

get.description: Returns a single recycle-bin record identified by the path parameter `record_id`. The record_id in the URL takes precedence over any filters or ids given in query parameters.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Identifier of the recycle-bin record to retrieve.

200.description: Successful response with the requested data.

display_name.description: Display name of the deleted record.

deleted_time.description: Timestamp when the record was deleted.

module.description: Module information of the deleted record.

name.description: Name of the user who deleted.

id.description: Identifier of the user who deleted.
Unique identifier of the recycle bin record.

more_records.description: Indicates if more records are available.

400.description: Bad request — invalid parameters. See response body for error details.

delete.description: Permanently deletes the recycle-bin record identified by `record_id`. This is irreversible.

parameters.name: recordId
parameters.description: Identifier of the recycle-bin record to delete.

200.description: Successful response with the requested count.

400.description: Bad request — invalid parameters or payload. See response body for error details.

Filters.name: filters
Filters.description: Filter expression to scope records. For complex filters use the POST /actions/restore requestBody `filters` object instead; this query param typically accepts a simple criteria string or encoded expression.

ResultItem.description: Individual result item with status and details.

details.description: Optional additional details about the item

ResultList.description: List of results from the operation.
